title: Inside the Machine: AI Unpacked

modules.title: The Robot That Guesses Your Lunch
modules.sub: Discover how AI makes predictions from limited information
modules.description: Learners step into Nadia's school and encounter the AI recommendation system firsthand, noticing something odd: everyone keeps getting the same suggestions. Through a guided guessing game, they begin to explore what inputs an AI might use to make decisions and why those guesses can feel surprisingly off.

modules.title: Training Day: Teaching with Data
modules.sub: See how data shapes everything an AI can learn
modules.description: Learners explore how an AI is trained by feeding it example data and watching how its answers change. They make choices about which data to include and discover that the quality and variety of training examples directly affects how well the AI performs.

modules.title: Patterns, Patterns Everywhere
modules.sub: Uncover how AI spots hidden patterns in everyday information
modules.description: Learners run a series of sorting experiments to see how AI systems detect patterns across images, words, and numbers. By trying to find patterns themselves before revealing what the AI found, they develop an intuition for why pattern recognition is both powerful and prone to surprising mistakes.

modules.title: Rules vs. Learning: The Big Difference
modules.sub: Compare systems that follow rules to systems that learn
modules.description: Learners put two types of AI side by side — one that follows a fixed set of rules and one that learns from data — and test them against tricky scenarios. They decide which approach works better in different situations and begin building vocabulary to explain the difference to someone else.

modules.title: When the Machine Gets It Wrong
modules.sub: Investigate what causes AI errors and who they affect
modules.description: Learners analyse real-style case studies where AI systems produced unfair or incorrect outputs, including scenarios close to Nadia's own school situation. They identify what went wrong in the training data or design and reflect on the real-world consequences of AI mistakes for different groups of people.

modules.title: Build a Mini Classifier Yourself
modules.sub: Train your own simple AI model and test its limits
modules.description: Learners build and train a basic image or text classifier using a hands-on tool, making decisions about what data to feed it and how to label examples. After testing their model, they reflect on what they would change to make it fairer or more accurate, connecting their experience back to Nadia's story.

synopsis: Follow Nadia, a student who discovers her school's new AI recommendation system is giving everyone the same suggestions â and sets out to understand why by pulling apart how AI actually thinks. This course uses hands-on experiments and story choices to demystify machine learning from the inside out.
rationale: Most youth AI content shows students what AI does without ever explaining how it works, leaving a conceptual gap that makes it impossible for them to reason critically about AI decisions they encounter in real life.
learning_outcome: Students can describe in their own words how an AI system learns from data, identify situations where that learning could go wrong, and explain the difference between rule-based and learning-based systems to a peer.
